name: Italian Cheese Terrine
recipeIngredient: 9 1 oz slices Muenster or Mozzarella cheese, divided
recipeInstructions:
1. Beat the cream cheese and butter together in a bowl using an electric mixer until smooth.
2. Add the Parmesan cheese and pesto; beat until well combined. Set aside to cool slightly.
3. Line a 3-cup serving bowl with plastic wrap, leaving enough overhang on all sides.
4. Diagonally cut each Muenster or Mozzarella slice in half. Arrange them in a pinwheel pattern inside the lined bowl, overlapping slightly.
5. Spread half of the cream cheese mixture evenly over the cheese layers.
6. Spoon and spread half of the Basil-Tomato Sauce over the cream cheese layer.
7. Cut the remaining slices of Muenster or Mozzarella into triangles.
8. Arrange the triangle pieces on top of the tomato sauce, then add more cream cheese mixture.
9. Repeat with additional tomato sauce and triangle pieces of cheese.
10. Fold the plastic wrap tightly around all layers to secure them.
11. Place a heavy object, such as a small cast-iron skillet, on top to compact the terrine.
12. Refrigerate for at least 8 hours or up to 3 days before serving.
13. When ready to serve, carefully invert the terrine onto a cheese board or plate and remove the plastic wrap.
14. Garnish with fresh herb sprigs if desired, and serve with crackers or baguette slices.
This recipe yields approximately 3 cups of Italian Cheese Terrine.
